You're running the paid ads, you're creating the content, you're just not getting a high enough percentage of people off that.
Two is slow response, so people just not reaching out when leads actually do come in to the CRM.
Three, and this one is actually a big one, even on the marketing conversion, is unqualified leads clogging up your pipeline.
So they do fill out the form, but they're not qualified.
They do talk to the chat, but they get to the wrong salesperson.

Like, I want to run this thing.
I don't know what's going to happen quite yet.
And there's ways you can kind of like create some boundaries and this and that.
But that usually is not a mindset that facilitates growth and experimentation.
And that's not really where their value is.

So I think the reason we're able to do that is, you know, most like creative AI, like if I just want to go riff, you know, a white paper, I may spend all day just pulling tokens and running the LLM and like end up with, I may spend 10 minutes.
may spend all day.
Our AI has objectives and a mission.
And so we're kind of able to predict like roughly how long that's going to take to kind of get each lead worked and moved.
So that helps.
And then I think the second thing is we also value off of incremental meetings.
So basically, like.
When we come into an account, we'll look at their traffic.
That will definitely be a, do they have the problem?
We'll say, okay, you have 22,000 site visitors.
What's your conversion?
It's typically between 3% and 5%.
Cool.
If we started conversations with the other 95%, do you think I could get you 5, 10 extra qualified meetings?
Again, I can do that over 20,000 people, and I just need to get 5.
Cool.
What's your ACV?
Oh, it's 15 grand.
Cool.
I'm 2K.
So like you get 15 grand, I get 2K.
Do we believe that if we talk to the 95% of people that aren't filling out your forms, we could get that going?
And that tends to be how we kind of go in.

And then what we do is we say 60 days, opt out.
So you can opt out anytime in the first 60 days.

And then, like I said, they're just like, you know,

Some customers, just grow so fast, and now they're churning out, you know, they go from 20,000 in site traffic to 100,000, and we just let them know, like, look, if you guys surge past 20% of this cap, like, we'll need to reassess, and this is what that will look like.
And most of them are like, yeah, got it.
We'll get there when we get there.
Got it, got it.
Super helpful.
Well, I can certainly take this back with the team, try to figure out what are some good starting places.
As far as, like, you know, what we were talking about earlier of whether it is experimenting with what we do and then take that to our customers.
It's a valid path.
I don't know if it's one that we transparently, like, have, like, the true bandwidth for.

If anything, our flaw now to a fault is putting all our bandwidth towards our customers, and there's some, like, we, you know, the event we put on and things like that, like, you know, we're doing some things for sure on the marketing side, but we also.
And, you know, are fortunate enough to have, like, some pretty strong pipeline.
And so, you know, we're kind of putting time and attention towards, like, okay, what are the, you know, if you folks, like, I don't know if you met Jason, but he leads our go-to-market side.
He's certainly doing a lot of variety of things, but he's also spending probably, like, the vast majority of his time on, like, our accounts.
So, you know, that's probably the place that makes sense.
And then it's a matter of, like, who is that right person?
And how do we structure that test and things of that nature?
I'm not exactly sure who the right one is at this moment, but no doubt there's going to be a whole bunch we can consider testing.
I mean, if I think about those that, especially just given our, like, relationship with them, and actually they were at the event, co-host of the event with us is the WebStacks team.
They're doing all sorts of, like, really interesting things.
And that's, I don't know if you've talked with them at all.
but...
but...
No,

They're also an agency that does web development, and so our customers since August of last year, they're also, you know, they have this interesting approach where they're, you know, thinking in a bit of a growth mindset, even though they specifically are more like their bread and butter is creating websites and web experiences.
They also, like, have folks that come in and run their own growth programs, their own demand gen programs.

They work with us, and anyways, they're doing all sorts of interesting things that I could see plugging into this, and they're at a phase where traffic has been steady, definitely showing impact to conversion, but we feel like we're just, like, scratching the surface there in a lot of ways.

And so, yeah, I think that could be an interesting, like, compound effect of what you're trying to do, because if that's, like, someone who would be, they just have the mindset and knowledge of, like, how to be a good partner and how to experiment with something like this.
It's alongside us, but also could be an inroads to other customers as well, especially given where they play and managing the web experience and things like that.
I think that's a perfect next step.
Would you be open to helping get that meeting?

I know Jesse there quite well.

honestly, I have a bit of a nightmare customer.

Yeah, Score, S-C-H-O-R.
But yeah, I'll reach out to Jesse.
Perfect.
And actually, you know, shoot an intro as well, but I'm...
Pretty sure he would be game to test something like this out.
So he's always doing all sorts of fun stuff.
was walking me through all this, like, really interesting stuff.

set up an MCP server with that.
They're leveraging that a whole variety of ways.
And so it's just, like, to me, that's, like, the kind of person who would really lean into this type of stuff.
That's perfect.
Because I think, ultimately, like, we see ourselves being the tech partner for creatives and agencies.
Like, we don't, the art of the customer, I think, will still be owned at the agency level, right?
You know the end-to-end customer journey of insert name of customer that you're trying to drive.
We really want to come along as the AI tech partner to say, we're going to give you the best-in-class conversational AI tech to go deliver that journey.
So, yeah, I think they make a, they're a perfect partner there, right?
Because we're getting pulled into a lot of service asks.
Like, oh, can you help me with this?
Can you, it is, like, they, people forget.
Like, we're, like, we're, we're.
software company.
But to your point, our customers are like, but how do I fix my messaging?
And I'm like, go talk to Kyle, right?
That's not me.
Like, I know what you should be doing.
So I think that's another reason why we're getting pulled there is like, we should probably think about sending some of our customers.

But like, we'll still need outlets to send some of our bigger customers because we again, we want to be we want to give you the best in class conversational conversion AI.
But then we want the artists to play on our palette, if you will.
Yeah, I think there's a lot of cool ways to have this can eventually feed into, you know, bi directionally into the different plays of like, just rich context that helps on both ends.
And what that looks like, you know, I'm not yet sure.
But, you know, having a richer understanding of who these people are, and how that feeds into our constant strategy is super, super important.
But we're also synthesizing a lot of like qualitative, quantitative things that aren't always going to be as like obvious that are going to be somewhere referenceable in the way your system could.
So I could see there being some interesting like, what is that?
What are some things to build off of there in some fun ways?
yeah, when we first chatted, that was like, yeah, a variety of different things that got my gears through.
And I'm like, OK, this would be cool.
Yeah.
And again, we like to build.
So we like to tag on to people that have cool ideas and build them out and see them work.
So I think the web stacks with Jesse would be a great, to your point, like three points of a triangle.
Totally.
Get something cool there and then go from there.
I wish I had this aha moment when we were all together at the happy hour and I could have grabbed him.
But I think by the time you and I were chatting, Jesse might have headed up.
But anyways, I will get the connection happening nonetheless.
Appreciate the walkthrough.
Cool.
And let me know if there's intros you need for my end or.
Customers, we should send your way.
Happy to.
Happy to.
Yeah.
Like I said, I love it.
And I also, I like that there's one actionable project, right?
People can always talk.
Well, you do all this cool stuff.
And it's like, cool, what's one thing?

There's so much you can do.
That's not really helpful unless you kind of like start somewhere and do something, right?
But we run into that all the time sometimes.

And we're like, oh, what about this?
It's like, oh, sure.
Maybe.
don't.
Oh, my God.

So like at first we were like sitting there like, okay, so you talk to it and it talks back to you.

And then after the open AI, they were like, so you have a genie that just gives me customers.
And you're like, what the hell?
Like, you know, last week I couldn't even explain like you talking to it.
And this week I just make leads love you.

all right, my friend, well, I know we're over.

Well, I hope the conference, conference or conferences.

Fun, fun.
Happy summiting.
Hope it goes well.
I asked if Mike could attend, because I've been traveling so much, and I've been focused on the customer events, and I'm the CEO, and they politely reminded me that I'm the CEO.
Cool.
So that's going to be a no.
Got it.
Cool.
See you there.
Enjoy.
Enjoy.
Well, I'm sure it will be fruitful.

Like, I feel like the CEO part is going to be awesome.
Talking to my other founders, so great.
I feel like sitting there, just listening to them be like, so what we're seeing in the market, I'm like, yeah, I mean, F-ing, no.
Yeah, yeah, that can definitely be mind-numbing for sure.
Luckily, I feel like we've been blessed with Karan, who's our lead investor.
He's very refreshing as far as VCs go, and I think he just gets it at a level that I haven't seen as frequently.
So luckily, whenever he shares stuff with us, we're like, damn, I want like 10 more of those things, you know, so he's a rare breed.
Yeah, we get a lot of like, hey, you know, it's really competitive in the AI go-to-market space right now.
I'm like, mm-hmm.
Wow.
Yeah, I would be curious to- I had no idea.
Yeah, that and GEO.
We certainly hear a lot, but...
Oh, the AEO thing that's now?

Cool, man.

truly hypothetical things.

But very interesting trends, but I think the thing to keep in mind is it's still a fraction of overall traffic.
Correct.
so naturally, yes, a lot of things are shifting and this and that, but people love to play around with this data.

So we have to talk about it more and more.
And I mean, there's legit things there.
Don't get me wrong.
Like it does disproportionately convert at a higher rate.
That's all great.

base is still, yeah, your base is still.
I mean, I think that's the way for us.

I mean, it
It works to a degree for businesses where it worked already.
You know what I mean?

You're not going to take a business that had no cold email presence and magically change that.

No, it's not your silver bullet.

Well, it's the vaporware that kills me.

Well, he was literally from a competitor.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Discuss potential partnership between growthx.ai and synapsa.ai, exploring how synapsa's AI-powered conversational tools could benefit growthx's customers.](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [synapsa.ai offers AI-powered conversational tools for mid-funnel engagement, improving marketing conversion and lead qualification](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=231.0)
  - [Pricing is $2,000/month, replacing a warm inbound SDR with set tiers and usage caps](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1863.0)
  - [growthx.ai sees potential value but needs to carefully consider implementation strategy](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1736.0)
  - [WebStacks (growthx customer) identified as potential first test case for partnership](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2142.0)

## Topics

### synapsa.ai Product Overview

  - [AI agents trained on company knowledge for conversational engagement](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1255.0)
  - [Identifies and engages warm leads through web chat, email, and text](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=885.0)
  - [De-anonymizes 30-40% of site traffic at contact level](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=892.0)
  - [Adapts page experiences based on user data](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=969.0)
  - [Manages entire meeting experience (booking, reminders, recording)](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1152.0)
  - [Integrates with CRMs like HubSpot and Salesforce for data mapping](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1296.0)

### Implementation Considerations

  - [Can ingest existing customer personas/archetypes for more targeted conversations](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1432.0)
  - [Agencies can get certified to deploy and manage synapsa for their clients](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1825.0)
  - [synapsa aims to be tech partner for agencies, not replace strategy/creative](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2302.0)
  - [60-day opt-out period before annual commitment](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2029.0)

### Potential Partnership Structure

  - [growthx could experiment internally first or go directly to customer test case](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=1770.0)
  - [WebStacks identified as good potential first joint customer to trial with](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2142.0)
  - [Opportunity for bi-directional data enrichment between synapsa and growthx](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2376.0)

### Market/Industry Discussion

  - [Challenges of AI hype vs. reality (e.g. AEO, cold outreach)](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2736.0)
  - [Frustration with "vaporware" and exaggerated claims in AI space](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2860.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Kyle to reach out to Jesse at WebStacks about potential synapsa trial](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2240.0)
  - [Maddie to follow up Monday to coordinate WebStacks introduction](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2957.0)
  - [Explore specific use case and experiment structure for initial test](https://fathom.video/share/1FhG2X-y_PFhNCG-EseCzRpszAaox6ms?tab=summary&timestamp=2479.0)

action_items.description: Reach out to Jesse Score from WebStacks re potential Synapsa.ai partnership/test
Follow up w/ Kyle on Mon re Jesse/WebStacks intro; prep for potential mtg
